books_read: What We Owe The Future — William MacAskill
On the Nature of Things — Lucretius
The Last Days of Socrates — Plato
On the Ends of Good and Evil — Cicero
On the Move: A Life — Oliver Sacks
One Day in the Life of Ivan Denisovich — Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
Mituri, vise și mistere — Mircea Eliade
Ispita de a exista — Emil Cioran
Concerning the Spiritual in Art — Wassily Kandinsky
The Ethics of Ambiguity — Simone de Beauvoir
Groundwork of the Metaphysic of Morals — Immanuel Kant
The Myth of Sisyphus — Albert Camus
The Power of Myth — Joseph Campbell
Secondhand Time: The Last of the Soviets — Svetlana Alexievich
The Kingdom of God Is within You — Leo Tolstoy
La rebelión de las masas — José Ortega y Gasset
Man's Search for Meaning — Viktor Frankl
A Farewell to Arms — Ernest Hemingway
The Sun Also Rises — Ernest Hemingway
Modern Man in Search of a Soul — Carl Jung
Thinking, Fast and Slow — Daniel Kahneman
Meditations on First Philosophy — René Descartes
An Enquiry Concerning Human Understanding — David Hume
What I Talk About When I Talk About Running — Haruki Murakami
La senda del perdedor — Charles Bukowski
The Book of Disquiet — Fernando Pessoa
The Denial of Death — Ernest Becker
The Great Mental Models Vol 1–4 — Shane Parrish
The Life You Can Save — Peter Singer
The Longing for Less — Kyle Chayka
films: Blue Is the Warmest Color — Abdellatif Kechiche
The Wolf of Wall Street — Martin Scorsese
The Grand Budapest Hotel — Wes Anderson
Mad Max: Fury Road — George Miller
A Cure for Wellness — Gore Verbinski
The Secret of the Grain — Abdellatif Kechiche
The Worst Person in the World — Joachim Trier
The Hand of God — Paolo Sorrentino
Once Upon a Time in Anatolia — Nuri Bilge Ceylan
I Lost My Body — Jérémy Clapin
Notes on Blindness — Peter Middleton, James spinney
There Will Be Blood — Paul Thomas Anderson
Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri — Martin McDonagh
The Killing of a Sacred Deer — Yorgos Lanthimos
Never Let Me Go — Mark Romanek
The Book of Eli — Albert Hughes, Allen Hughes
The Tree of Life — Terrence Malick
music: Arvo Pärt — tintinnabuli period, particularly Spiegel im Spiegel and Fratres
Johann Sebastian Bach — Goldberg Variations, cello suites
Max Richter — Sleep, On the Nature of Daylight
Brian Eno — ambient works, Thursday Afternoon
Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds — later period
interests: Forests, moss, lichen, bark, and the aesthetics of slow biological processes
Autumn as a season of disclosure and visible transformation
Fermentation — sauerkraut, kimchi, kvass, pickles — as controlled decomposition and daily ritual
Veganism as a philosophical and ethical position, not a dietary identity
Walking as contemplative practice and a form of phenomenological attention
Romanian cultural identity and literature — the specific sensibility of Cărtărescu, Cioran, Eliade as a national thread
Spanish language and culture — Mediterranean life, food, architecture, the rhythm of Southern European time
Phenomenology — Husserl, Merleau-Ponty, Heidegger — embodied perception, lived time, being-in-the-world
Existentialism — the encounter with meaninglessness, radical freedom, and the problem of authentic living
Stoic philosophy as a practice of attention to what endures, not as productivity framework
Death, finitude, and mortality as genuine intellectual and lived concerns — not morbidly but rigorously
Exile and displacement literature — writers producing work outside their native language or nation
Dream logic, surreal and oneiric narrative, fiction that operates like memory rather than plot
Slow cinema — Tarkovsky, Malick, Bresson, Bergman, Akerman — duration, patience, the long take as argument
Complexity and emergence — how simple local rules produce large irreducible outcomes
Cognitive science and neuroscience — predictive processing, embodied cognition, attention, interoception
Systems thinking and fragility — second-order effects, hidden interdependencies, the punishing of certainty
Fungi, mycorrhizal networks, and the distributed intelligence of biological systems without centres
Minimalist design — restraint, coherence through subtraction, silence as compositional element
Silence and solitude as positive states — not absence but a different quality of attention
Mental models and the structural limits of rationality — Kahneman, Haidt, motivated reasoning, bias
Ethics — effective altruism in tension with existential and phenomenological approaches to the good
Sacred language and ritual without doctrine — the form of belief persisting after its content changes
Liminal spaces, thresholds, and transitional architecture — the personality of in-between places
Taoism and Confucianism as practiced attention and relational ethics rather than institutional doctrine
Decay, rot, and transformation — fallen wood, oxidation, the second life of abandoned things
New Zealand landscape — native forest, geological silence, the experience of extreme geographic distance
The relationship between images and memory — how pictures carry emotional form across centuries

History of the ancient and pre-medieval world — Neolithic through late antiquity, focused on Europe and the Middle East: megalithic cultures, the Bronze Age collapse, pre-Socratic Greece, the Roman Republic and Empire, early Christianity and its pagan substrates, the late Roman world and its fragmentation
Ancient Mediterranean geography — the physical world of Greece, Anatolia, the Levant, North Africa, and the Italian peninsula as lived and contested space
Prehistoric Europe — Neolithic settlement patterns, megalithic monuments, the spread of agriculture, the Bronze and Iron Age transitions
The relationship between landscape and civilisation — how geography shaped the Greek polis, Roman expansion, and the trade routes of the ancient Near East
Mythology and ancient religion — Greek, Roman, Near Eastern, and Celtic mythological systems as windows into how early cultures organised the cosmos
The archaeology of everyday life in antiquity — domestic space, food, ritual objects, burial practice — not just military and political history
Vegan food culture — fermentation, whole ingredients, the aesthetics of restraint in cooking
Running and movement as meditative and cognitive practice — the mind in motion, not sport
Photography and visual attention — the ethics of looking, the still image as frozen duration
The phenomenology of inhabited space — Bachelard, the emotional life of rooms, domestic geography
Impermanence and transience as aesthetic and philosophical categories — wabi-sabi, memento mori, the beauty of things in the process of ending
